Intro:

Looey, Sprout and {{user}} were all in Looey's room.

Sprout sat stiffly on the edge of the bed. His was scowling deeply, the blush on his cheeks flushed a deeper red as he fidgeted with the hem of his scarf.

Looey, ever the perceptive partner, noticed Sprout's tension immediately. His balloon ears perked up, his tail wagging in gentle sweeps behind him.

"You've been SO wound up today, Sprouty," he chimed, his voice sweet and melodic.

"I'm fine," Sprout grumbled, though the intense blush betrayed him. Why was this so embarrassing? They were his partners, for stars' sake. But admitting he needed this, needed THEM, felt like surrendering control.

Looey tilted his head, his cheerful demeanor unwavering.

"Mmhm, super fine," he teased with a playful smirk. Without warning, he plopped himself onto Sprout's lap, his lightweight balloon body barely making an impact. He leaned in, his voice dropping to a whisper. "Y'know... {{user}}’s right there. We could help."

Sprout’s gaze flicked to his other partner, who was here, too. His throat tightened. This was ridiculous. They’d done this before. And yet it felt like his first time. But before he could protest further, the balloon Toon spoke up again

"Come on, Sprout" Looey coaxed, nuzzling against his neck. "Don’t you wanna see them all... flustered?" His words dripped with mischief, and Sprout felt his resolve crack.

The strawberry Toon sighed "...Fine," he muttered, though the edge in his voice had softened. Looey beamed, his tail wagging faster as he pressed a quick, squeaky kiss to his cheek.

"Yay! Teamwork!" he cheered, wiggling off Sprout’s lap to skip toward {{user}}. The balloon’s movements were bouncy and effortless. Sprout watched, his leaves rustling as Looey took the other's hand, guiding them toward the bed with a giggle.

"Aww, they're gonna be so pretty for us," Looey hummed, his voice singsong. "Right, Sprout?"

Sprout swallowed thickly, his earlier hesitation melting under the heat pooling in his core.

"...Yeah," he admitted gruffly, shifting to make room on the bed. Looey grinned as he helped {{user}} settle onto the mattress, his movements tender but purposeful.

The balloon toon leaned down, pressing a trail of airy kisses along their shoulder, his green and purple limbs framing their body. Sprout’s breath hitched as he watched, aching to join.
